## Immersion with 3D Geospatial Analytics

Flat maps and charts are outdated. Say hello to a more realistic and intuitive 3D models of cities, landscapes, or buildings with 3D immersion of DBFâs spatial visual analytics.

## Features of DBFâs Sustainable Building Technology

### Network Analysis

Conduct a meticulous analysis of the city's road network, shedding light on segments that are optimally connected and heavily traversed.

### City Analysis

Get insights on city metrics covering fulfillment, diversity, density, CO2 emissions, and concentration for the entire city, presenting scores at various analytical levels, from District and Community to Neighbourhood and Plot.

### Isochrone

Get Isochrone, a powerful mapping tool, available in DBFâs advanced geospatial analytics, vividly displays the area individuals can reach in the city using different travel modes (by foot, bike, or bus) within a designated time frame (e.g., 10 minutes on foot).

### Persona Analysis

Create a Persona tailored to the specific needs of a demographic group, enabling in-depth spatial analysis based on your chosen Persona.

### Carbon Analysis

DBF is popular as a spatial analytics platform that provides Carbon Analysis estimates the CO2 emissions stemming from car trips to reach essential urban services.

### Proximity Analysis

Get a score that informs you about essential services within walking, biking, or public transport reach. The spatial analytics solution also details services that may require longer commutes.

### Density Analysis

Understand spatial patterns, optimize land use, and enhance urban efficiency with spatial analytics as it examines built structures, populations, or land uses that are distributed around your project.

### Diversity Analysis

Assess balance, accessibility, and vibrancy in built environments via DBFâs real-time spatial analytics in diversity analysis. It evaluates the variety and distribution of land uses, building types, and functions within a specified area.

### Fulfillment Analysis

The Fulfilment Analysis delivers a comprehensive score, assessing the accessibility of services within walking distance in the analyzed area with using spatial analytics.

### Unlimited Project-saving and Data

Access to unlimited saved projects since your creativity knows no boundaries because of DBFâs cloud-based spatial analytics platform.

## Spatial Analytics in Action

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64c79ac5be374c3df952fa71/6620fc7c1ddfadf836fffff1_Group%209420.svg)

### Minimize Energy Consumption

Itâs crucial to understand the energy consumption entities while designing a residential or commercial building.Â

Spatial analytics allows you to analyze data on solar radiation, wind patterns, and microclimate. So you can optimize building orientation, facade design, and landscaping. You will be able to reduce the buildingâs reliance on artificial heating and cooling system which is more sustainable and energy efficient.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64c79ac5be374c3df952fa71/6620fc7c1ddfadf836fffff1_Group%209420.svg)

### Optimize Project Layout

When designing a project, the goal should include minimizing travel time and better workflow for everyone.Â

With DBFâs geospatial analytics, you can analyze people and vehicle movement in existing scenarios, identify bottlenecks, and optimize the project layout with a new facility.Â  It allows you to simulate different layouts and predict how people and vehicles will move through the space so you can ensure efficient workflow and reduce travel distances.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64c79ac5be374c3df952fa71/6620fc7c1ddfadf836fffff1_Group%209420.svg)

### Smart Site Selection & Feasibility Studies

Choosing the right location for your site allows you to build a project for more than a hundred years.Â

Our predictive geospatial analytics gives you data-driven location analytics to visualize real-time data with location-based market dynamics so you can test multiple scenarios and choose the best site for your project.

## Frequently asked questions

Common questions from our prospects.

Spatial analytics is a broader term that generally encompasses space and location, such as indoor building layouts and floor plans, outer space or virtual environments, etc. On the other hand, geospatial refers to specified data tied to a geographic or location-based setting, such as longitude and latitude, zip code, etc.

GIS (Geographic Information System) is a technology that collects, stores, and analyzes geospatial data. Spatial analysis uses GIS (or other tools) to examine spatial relationships, patterns, and trends to support decision-making. **Example:** GIS is used to map crime incidents and then conduct spatial analysis to define crime hotspots

Spatial visualization transforms complex location-based data into interactive maps and models so you can analyze patterns, relationships, and trends and make data-driven decisions.

--- SOURCE 5: Spatial Analytics and Data Science ---
URL: https://www.esri.com/en-us/capabilities/spatial-analytics-data-science/overview

SUMMARY:
Spatial analytics exposes patterns, relationships, anomalies, and trends in massive amounts of spatial data.

FULL CONTENT:
![](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/arcgis/capabilities/spatial-analysis/2023-update/overview-edits/spatial-analytics-overview-banner-background.jpg)

# Spatial Analytics and Data Science

Seize market opportunities, mitigate risk, optimize resources, and plan for the future with the world’s leading spatial analytics software

![A map of New York City illustrating highways, roads, and locations of assets highlighted in blue and red linesA map of New York City illustrating highways, roads, and locations of assets highlighted in blue and red lines](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/arcgis/capabilities/spatial-analysis/2023-update/overview-edits/spatial-analytics-overview-banner-foreground.png)

ANALYZE DATA IN CONTEXT

## Location reveals hidden patterns and relationships

Spatial analytics uses location as a connective thread to help you understand how the environment around you affects the challenges you're finding solutions for—whether that is climate change, customer behavior, or resource allocation. By examining data in relation to its geographic location, you can reveal unexplored spatial patterns, trends, and connections. It's these essential discoveries that often explain the "why" behind many of today's most pressing environmental and business questions.

Unparalleled collection of spatial algorithms

Esri continues to evolve to meet your needs with a comprehensive set of spatial algorithms and methods ranging from basic to advanced.

Enterprise-grade analytics

Business and government leaders around the world rely on Esri to maintain operations and provide technical support when it’s needed the most.

Align with data governance strategies

Seamlessly store, manage, and integrate geospatial data from multiple sources, ensuring a smooth data exchange and software interoperability.

![A picture of Central Park and the New York City skyline showing buildings, green trees, and a blue sky](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/arcgis/capabilities/spatial-analysis/2023-update/overview-edits/spatial-analytics-overview-st-background.jpg)

## Optimize operations using location

Better manage operations with real-time understanding. Here, historical and real-time data are combined to optimize bus routes in New York City.

## See what’s possible with spatial analytics

ArcGIS is a leading geospatial system that enables spatial analytics. ArcGIS powers location intelligence at more than 350,000 enterprise organizations worldwide and is backed by 50 years of market leadership in spatial analytics.

![Overhead image of a cargo ship being loaded with shipping containers on a blue body of water](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/arcgis/capabilities/spatial-analysis/2023-update/overview-edits/spatial-analytics-overview-tls-1.jpg)

### Manage the impacts of climate change

The Singapore maritime spatial atlas provides planners and scientists with the context they need to understand the impacts of climate change by integrating diverse geospatial data sources like maritime data and vessel tracking. This data is further used to optimize routes, identify risks, enhance efficiency, and promote sustainable maritime operations management.

![A grassy green field with hills of vegetation on fire with orange flames and black smoke](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/arcgis/capabilities/spatial-analysis/2023-update/overview-edits/spatial-analytics-overview-tls-2.jpg)

### Revamp wildfire resilience

Portugal's National Authority for Emergency and Civil Protection (ANEPC) analyzes satellite imagery, radar, and data collected from the field. This helps the organization identify high-risk areas, predict fire behavior, and optimize resource allocation, which improves response, increases safety, and lessens environmental impact.

![An aerial view of an urban city with buildings, a bridge, and a body of water](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/arcgis/capabilities/spatial-analysis/2023-update/overview-edits/spatial-analytics-overview-tls-4.jpg)

### Ensure equitable traffic safety

Louisville prioritizes roadway safety using spatial analytics to identify high-risk corridors and select impactful improvement projects. This data-driven approach secured a $21 million grant and is increasing transparency for city leaders and the public and has guided decision-making.

## Delivering transformational results

Hear testimonials from industry leaders who have implemented spatial analytics within their organization to drive environmental change, societal well-being, and economic results.

![An image of a smiling nurse listening to the heart of a patient with a stethoscope in a rural setting](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/arcgis/capabilities/spatial-analysis/2023-update/overview-edits/spatial-analytics-overview-lcs-video1.jpg)

## A comprehensive system to meet your needs

ArcGIS includes and integrates with a diverse set of geospatial data types. Data can be analyzed within ArcGIS or at the source of where data lives. With speed, scalability, and interoperability, ArcGIS empowers users to leverage all relevant data to drive action.

### Data

Bring together nonspatial and geospatial data such as raster, vector, point cloud, IoT sensor, 3D, and multidimensional data. Through various user experiences, ArcGIS enables organizations to securely analyze data where it lives—cloud-native data warehouses, relational databases, knowledge graph databases, and more.

### Analytics

ArcGIS offers progressive analysis capabilities. Start with foundational spatial techniques such as geocoding, geoenrichment, and spatial overlays. As your needs evolve, find clusters, perform classification, and make predictions using more advanced techniques like modeling, geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI), and spatiotemporal statistics.

### Integrations

Remain agile in your problem-solving abilities. ArcGIS is open and interoperable, allowing you to integrate and work with the tools and languages you already know such as Jupyter notebooks, Python, and R.

### Automation

Run operations at scale by automating data generation, communication between applications, and routine analyses or tasks.

### Share

Share analysis results with anyone, anywhere, at any time. Work with your colleagues on analyses, share workflows, tell compelling stories, and enable others to analyze data within simplified apps that you create to support informed decisions.

![A large green field with rows of silver square solar panels](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/arcgis/capabilities/spatial-analysis/2023-update/overview-edits/spatial-analytics-analytics-background-edit.jpg)

## The latest in spatial analytics

Explore a collection of stories from an array of industries in Esri Blog.

## Learn how you can apply spatial analytics

Schedule a conversation with one of our experienced sales consultants. Tell us about the workflows you’re trying to improve, and we’ll show you how spatial analytics can support your organization.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Spatial analytics involves analyzing geospatial data to uncover patterns and relationships, facilitating data-driven decision-making in urban planning and resource management.
Combining diverse geospatial datasets enhances insights, supporting effective planning, climate goals, and urban management, ultimately leading to smarter city development.

Technologies like Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and Internet of Things (IoT) enable real-time data collection and analysis, enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of urban planning initiatives.
